title: Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 IA-11 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 IA-11 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.14.32: Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.32' running on ms:o365:management sign-ins with AuthenticationRequirement / SessionId if present, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24h
| search ApplicationId IN ("*high_risk_app_guid_1*","*high_risk_app_guid_2*")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by UserId, SessionId
| eval session_age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,1)
| where session_age_min>480
description: Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements.
value: Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements.

grandmaExplanation: Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:management` sign-ins with `AuthenticationRequirement` / `SessionId` if present.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Replace ApplicationId tokens with production GUIDs; (2) align session length to policy; (3) pair with CASB step-up events if available; (4) document exceptions for legacy apps.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24h
| search ApplicationId IN ("*high_risk_app_guid_1*","*high_risk_app_guid_2*")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by UserId, SessionId
| eval session_age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,1)
| where session_age_min>480
```

Understanding this SPL

**Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles (IA-11)** ‚Äî Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` sign-ins with `AuthenticationRequirement` / `SessionId` if present. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:management, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by UserId, SessionId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **session_age_min** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where session_age_min>480` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles (IA-11)** ‚Äî Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` sign-ins with `AuthenticationRequirement` / `SessionId` if present. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (long sessions), Single value (policy breaches), Timeline
